slides.title: A Juice Carton's Diary
slides.content:
A carton is a container made of paper and plastic. Juice comes in cartons!

A diary is a book where you write what happens each day.

In this unit, a juice carton tells us its story - from the factory to the recycling plant!
slides.tip: A carton can have a second life if we recycle it!

slides.title: The Carton's Week
slides.html:
Monday: I was born in a factory. A machine made me from paper and plastic. A worker filled me with orange juice.

Tuesday: A truck took me to a supermarket. I sat on a shelf with many other cartons.

Wednesday: A girl picked me up! She drank all my juice at lunchtime. Then she put me in the recycling bin.

Thursday: I went to a recycling plant. Big machines cut me into tiny pieces.

Friday: The factory used my paper to make a new notebook. I have a new life!

slides.title: Key Vocabulary
slides.content:
Carton - a container for juice or milk
Factory - a big building where things are made
Recycling plant - a place where old things are made into new things
Shelf - a flat surface in a shop where products sit
Pieces - small parts of something bigger

slides.title: Present vs Past Tense
slides.content:
We use the present tense for things happening NOW:

'I am a juice carton.' / 'The machine makes cartons.'

We use the past tense for things that ALREADY HAPPENED:

'I was born in a factory.' / 'The machine made me.'

am -> was / is -> was / make -> made / take -> took / drink -> drank
slides.tip: Present = now. Past = before, already finished.

slides.title: Example: Present or Past?
slides.problem:
Is the sentence in the present or the past?

1. The carton is on the shelf.
2. A girl drank the juice yesterday.
3. The factory makes new notebooks.
4. The truck took me to the shop.

slides.title: Telling the Time: O'clock
slides.content:
When the long hand points to 12, we say o'clock.

3:00 = 'It is three o'clock.'
8:00 = 'It is eight o'clock.'
12:00 = 'It is twelve o'clock.'

O'clock means the time is exact - no extra minutes!

slides.title: Quarter Past and Half Past
slides.content:
When the long hand points to 3, we say quarter past.

3:15 = 'It is quarter past three.'
A quarter = 15 minutes (a quarter of 60 minutes).

When the long hand points to 6, we say half past.

3:30 = 'It is half past three.'
Half = 30 minutes (half of 60 minutes).

slides.title: Quarter To
slides.content:
When the long hand points to 9, we say quarter to.

But be careful! We say the NEXT hour!

3:45 = 'It is quarter to four.' (NOT quarter to three!)
8:45 = 'It is quarter to nine.'

The long hand is almost at the top, so we say the next hour.
slides.tip: Quarter TO = 15 minutes BEFORE the next hour. Say the next hour!

slides.title: Example: What Time Is It?
steps: 4. 9:45 -> It is QUARTER TO ten. (next hour!)
slides.answer: five o'clock, quarter past seven, half past two, quarter to ten

slides.title: A Recycling Day
slides.html:
It was a special day at school - Recycling Day!

At nine o'clock, the teacher took the class to the recycling plant. They saw big machines cutting cartons into tiny pieces.

At quarter past ten, they learned how paper is recycled. Old cartons become new notebooks!

At half past eleven, they had lunch. At quarter to one, they went back to school.

'Today I learned that every carton can have a second life,' said Tom. 'I will always recycle!'

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🧃 Key words: carton, factory, recycling plant, shelf, pieces
⏰ Present tense = now (I am, it makes). Past tense = before (I was, it made)
🕐 O'clock = long hand on 12 (3:00 = three o'clock)
🕒 Quarter past = long hand on 3 (3:15 = quarter past three)
🕕 Half past = long hand on 6 (3:30 = half past three)
🕘 Quarter to = long hand on 9, say NEXT hour (3:45 = quarter to four)
